outputs.processing_chain: Text Input → Parser → CompositionData → {Playback (Synth), Export (MIDI File)}

text_parser.description: Parse LLM-generated jazz compositions into structured data
methods: parseHeader() - Extract TITLE, TEMPO, KEY, TIME, SWING, BARS
parseChords() - Parse bar-based chord progressions with durations

chord_expander.description: Convert chord symbols to MIDI pitch arrays
methods: expandChord() - Map chord symbol to MIDI pitches
parseNoteName() - Convert pitch names (C4, Eb5) to MIDI numbers
features: Support for common jazz chords: maj, min, 7, maj7, m7, m7b5, dim7, sus4, 9, 13
Fallback to major triad for unknown chords

midi_generator.description: Generate MIDI files from composition data
methods: createMidiFile() - Generate JUCE MidiFile from CompositionData
exportMIDI() - Write MIDI file to disk
beatsToTicks() - Convert beats to MIDI ticks (480 TPPQN)
features: Tempo and time signature meta events
Three export modes: Melody, Chords, Full

classes: SimplePianoVoice - Custom SynthesiserVoice with additive oscillators
features: ADSR envelope: attack 8ms, decay 800ms, sustain 0.4, release 0.5s
High shelf cut: -6dB at 3000Hz for vintage warmth

methods: playComposition() - Start playback from parsed composition
stopPlayback() - Stop playback and clear events
features: Schedule MIDI events from composition data

audio_thread: NO parsing, NO file I/O, NO allocations

implementation_notes: All DSP components implemented in single pass (complexity 2.0)
State persistence for composition text only
Piano synth uses JUCE Synthesiser API with custom voice
Text parser tolerant of LLM formatting variations
Chord expander supports common jazz vocabulary with fallback
MIDI generator creates Type 0 files (single track)
Transport system uses time-based scheduling (not sample-accurate)
